definition: VP's CMP_MAP gradient accumulates partial-credit feedback across chained parser or grammar comparisons, enabling assembly of deep program structures (namespace trees, DTD content models, parser state-machine transitions, automaton states) whose blocked condition is a pointer non-null or runtime state check rather than a direct byte equality; cmplog lacking the gradient cannot retain the intermediate structural progress.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An XML document with a namespaced start element where the element's qualified-name prefix is bound to a matching xmlns declaration, so that inside the namespace loop the interned pointers prefix and pref are identical AND URI is non-NULL (e.g. <p:e xmlns:p="some-uri">), driving line 2332's true side ret->ns = ns.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves the branch 9/10 with hitcount 5 on the true side; the winning seed is a structurally complete XML document beginning <?xml version="1.0"?> with a <!DOCTYPE declaration, the kind of namespaced/DTD-bearing input that reaches xmlNewNs and the prefix==pref identity test. The CMP_MAP gradient feedback that vpc adds over cmplog rewards Hamming/prefix progress on the many string and integer comparisons along the namespace-construction path (dict interning, URI != NULL, the xmlNewNs comparisons), giving partial-credit toward assembling a valid namespaced element.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks the branch 10/10 with hitcount 0 on the true side; its winning-shaped seeds keep the false side (T=0 F=33 at L2332) and never set ret->ns at 2333. cmplog has the same edge-bucket feedback as naive plus I2S, but no CMP_MAP gradient, so it gets no partial-credit signal toward the well-formed prefix-binding and stalls at the namespace loop.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance buckets per intercepted CMP) is the single technique vpc has and cmplog lacks. The blocker prefix == pref is a pointer-identity comparison whose operands are runtime-interned heap pointers with no input-byte pre-image, so input-to-state operand substitution (which both fuzzers have) cannot substitute its way across it; only the gradient feedback on the surrounding string/dict comparisons guides the fuzzer to build an input where the prefix interns to the same pointer as a namespace declaration.

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must complete a <!DOCTYPE ... [ ... ]> internal subset so xmlParseLookupInternalSubset finds its closing delimiter and returns nonzero.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A DTD whose internal subset opens with '<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "..."' followed by a '[' and is then driven far enough that xmlParseLookupInternalSubset locates the closing ']' / '>' delimiter of the subset; the parser must be in XML_PARSER_DTD state with a lookahead-complete internal subset, not merely an opened-but-unterminated one.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog seeds carry well-formed DTD prologs ('<?xml version="1.0"?>' then '<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/1...' / 'dtd://') and build up richer subset bodies (xlink namespace strings, multiple declarations), so the buffered lookahead reaches the internal-subset terminator and xmlParseLookupInternalSubset returns true, taking the false branch into xmlParseInternalSubset.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog seeds stall on near-miss DTD bytes ('dtds~1', 'S[STEM', 'DOCTYPE\n', 'dt.s=1') that either corrupt the DOCTYPE keyword or never complete the internal subset, so xmlParseLookupInternalSubset keeps returning 0 (true branch, goto done) and the parser re-enters the loop without progressing.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient feedback on top of the I2S that both fuzzers already share; the gradient rewards inputs that get incrementally closer to satisfying the byte-comparison-driven lookahead inside xmlParseLookupInternalSubset, giving the winner a smooth path to a complete internal subset that cmplog (edge-bucket feedback only) cannot climb.

analysis.summary_one_line: An xmlns namespace declaration whose name has no matching DTD attribute declaration, reached through the multi-byte 'xmlns' prefix-match cascade in SAX2.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An XML document with a DTD (DOCTYPE/SYSTEM) and a namespace declaration (xmlns:prefix) whose attribute name is NOT declared in the DTD's attribute table, so the xmlGetDtdQAttrDesc/xmlGetDtdAttrDesc lookups all return NULL and xmlValidateOneNamespace sees attrDecl == NULL. Reaching the validation call first requires passing the byte-exact 'xmlns' prefix comparison cascade (att[0..4] == 'x','m','l','n','s') in the SAX2 attribute path.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves the branch in 8/10 trials. Its winning seeds carry a DOCTYPE/SYSTEM DTD and reach xmlValidateOneNamespace with attrDecl NULL: on the d=1 chain the blocker line L4660 shows W taking both sides (T=9 F=9) while the loser only ever takes the false side (T=0 F=27). The CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards partial matches on the multi-byte 'xmlns' equality cascades, letting the winner climb att[0]=='x'..att[4]=='s' (L1235/L1236, L1724/L1725) which W hits (T=18 F=0 / T=9 F=0) but the loser stalls on (T=6 F=6, T=0 F=0).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog resolves the branch in 0/10 trials, blocked in 8. cmplog has the same I2S mutator but lacks value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient, so it cannot accumulate partial-credit feedback on the conjunctive 'xmlns' byte comparisons: at L1724 the loser is T=0 F=0 (never reaches the test true) and at L1235 it is split T=6 F=6, never driving the namespace-validation call where attrDecl == NULL can be observed taking its true side (L4660 loser T=0 F=27).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance buckets on intercepted CMPs) is the single technique delta between value_profile_cmplog (winner) and cmplog (loser); both already have the same input-to-state substitution mutator, so that comparison-solving capability is held constant. The gradient gives partial-match feedback on the multi-byte 'xmlns' equality cascade, letting the winner assemble the namespace-decl path that the loser cannot.
